title: Same-Lap Learning-to-Rank Expert Instead Of Only Binary Classification
summary: One prior research direction was same-lap ranking, and the public docs support query-group ranking with binary labels.
rationale: One prior research direction was same-lap ranking, and the public docs support query-group ranking with binary labels. This task is naturally partly relative: on a given lap, the model should rank which cars are most likely to pit next, not just score them independently.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost `rank:pairwise` or `rank:ndcg` model with `qid=(Year, Race, LapNumber)` and/or a CatBoost ranking model with group IDs set to race-lap. Keep the current classifier as the main model, then blend the ranking expert’s OOF scores with binary and survival outputs; ranking features should emphasize same-lap relative deltas and opponent-aware signals.
expected_effect: Usually modest standalone CV, but valuable ensemble diversity because the objective optimizes within-lap ordering rather than global calibration.
risk: Many query groups are small and may yield noisy pair construction; ranking predictions also need a secondary calibrator or blend because raw scores are not probabilities.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
